name: AWS (SageMaker)
tagline: SAS wins on governed analytics, industry solutions, and business-user accessibility beyond developer tooling

overview.whatTheyDo: Amazon Web Services provides SageMaker (ML platform), Bedrock (GenAI), QuickSight (BI), and 200+ cloud services. ML offering is developer-focused with notebook-driven workflows, AutoML, and model hosting.
overview.sweetSpot: Cloud-native organizations already on AWS wanting ML capabilities without leaving their ecosystem. Developer-heavy teams comfortable with Python/notebooks. Startups and digital-native companies.
overview.typicalBuyer: CTO, VP Engineering, Head of Data Platform, Cloud Architect
overview.phPresence: Strong PH presence through AWS Philippines team, local partners (Trend Micro, Cloud Kinetics, etc.), and AWS Marketplace. Many PH enterprises already on AWS for infrastructure.
overview.recentMomentum: SageMaker Canvas (no-code ML), Bedrock (GenAI), SageMaker Unified Studio. AWS investing heavily in making ML accessible to broader audience.
overview.pricingModel: Consumption-based. SageMaker costs are compute + storage + inference endpoints. Can be bundled into existing AWS committed spend (EDP). Appears low entry but scales unpredictably.

whereWeWin.headline: Production ML Governance for Regulated Industries
whereWeWin.explanation: SAS Model Manager provides formal model lifecycle management with approval workflows, regulatory documentation, and champion/challenger testing designed for BSP/IC examination. SageMaker Model Registry tracks models but lacks formal governance chains for regulated industries.
whereWeWin.proofPoint: No Philippine bank has passed BSP examination using SageMaker alone for model governance. SAS has been the standard for BSP model documentation.

whereWeWin.headline: Pre-Built Industry Solutions
whereWeWin.explanation: SAS delivers production-ready fraud detection, AML, risk management, and clinical analytics with domain expertise built in. AWS provides generic ML building blocks — customers must build industry solutions from scratch or find third-party ISVs.
whereWeWin.proofPoint: SAS Fraud Management has pre-built detection models for Philippine payment fraud patterns. On AWS, you'd need to build this from raw SageMaker components.

whereWeWin.headline: Business User Analytics (Not Just Developers)
whereWeWin.explanation: SAS Visual Analytics and Model Studio enable non-programmers to build and consume analytics. SageMaker is fundamentally a developer tool — Canvas is improving but limited compared to SAS's mature visual interfaces.
whereWeWin.proofPoint: 60% of SAS Viya users are non-programmers. SageMaker usage is >90% developer/data scientist personas.

whereTheyWin.whyItMatters: If customer is already paying millions for AWS infrastructure, adding SageMaker is frictionless — no new vendor, no new procurement, can use existing committed spend.
whereTheyWin.howToNeutralize: SAS runs ON AWS (EKS). Not asking customer to leave AWS — asking them to add the governance and industry layer that SageMaker lacks. SAS on AWS Marketplace simplifies procurement.

whereTheyWin.whyItMatters: Bedrock provides access to Claude, Llama, Titan models with enterprise guardrails. Easy to prototype GenAI use cases within existing AWS environment.
whereTheyWin.howToNeutralize: SAS adds governance FOR GenAI — model cards for LLMs, output quality monitoring, bias detection. Position: use Bedrock for GenAI experiments, govern with SAS for production.

whereTheyWin.whyItMatters: SageMaker cost can be absorbed into existing Enterprise Discount Program. SAS requires separate budget line.
whereTheyWin.howToNeutralize: Compare TCO: SageMaker compute + hiring ML engineers to build fraud/AML from scratch + governance consulting vs. SAS purpose-built solution. The build cost often exceeds SAS license.

features.notes: SageMaker Model Registry lacks BSP-grade governance workflows
Canvas is improving but narrow vs. SAS VA + Model Studio
AWS Fraud Detector exists but is basic rules-based, not enterprise-grade
SAS Intelligent Decisioning vs. custom Lambda/SageMaker setup
AWS owns the infra layer — SAS runs on top
Bedrock is comprehensive; SAS adds governance for GenAI
Both strong — SAS adds governance to AutoML output
SageMaker Studio is purpose-built for notebooks
SAS dedicated forecasting vs. SageMaker DeepAR
SAS Model Manager vs. SageMaker Model Monitor — SAS has regulatory context
SAS runs anywhere; SageMaker is AWS-only

landmines.question: If you needed to move to Azure or GCP in 3 years, what would that migration look like for your ML workloads?
landmines.expectedAnswer: SageMaker is AWS-only — significant rework required for multi-cloud
landmines.whatToSayNext: SAS runs identically on AWS, Azure, GCP, or on-prem. Zero code changes. Your analytics aren't locked to one cloud provider.

landmines.question: Can your compliance team produce BSP-format model documentation from SageMaker?
landmines.expectedAnswer: No native BSP documentation format — would need custom development
landmines.whatToSayNext: SAS Model Manager auto-generates documentation in the format BSP examiners expect. Critical for Circular 1112 compliance.

landmines.question: How would your business analysts — not data scientists — build and validate a fraud detection model?
landmines.expectedAnswer: SageMaker requires coding — Canvas is limited for complex models
landmines.whatToSayNext: SAS enables business users to build, validate, and monitor models visually. That's how you scale analytics beyond the data science team.

objections.objection: We're already on AWS — SageMaker is included
objections.whyTheySayIt: Path of least resistance. No new vendor, no new budget request. SageMaker feels 'free' within AWS spend.
objections.response: SAS runs ON AWS — we're not asking you to leave. The question is: what does SageMaker give you for regulated analytics? Show me the model governance workflow, the BSP-format documentation, the pre-built fraud detection. Those aren't included — you'd build them from scratch at significant cost.
proofPoints: SAS available on AWS Marketplace for simplified procurement
Building fraud/AML from SageMaker components costs $2-5M in engineering
objections.whatNotToSay: Don't say 'SageMaker can't do ML' — it absolutely can. Focus on the regulated industry gap.

objections.objection: AWS has better GenAI with Bedrock
objections.whyTheySayIt: GenAI hype cycle. Executives want AI capabilities and AWS Bedrock is accessible.
objections.response: Bedrock is excellent for GenAI experimentation and prototyping. For production GenAI in regulated industries, you need governance: model cards, output monitoring, bias detection, audit trails. SAS provides the governance layer FOR GenAI — including governing Bedrock outputs.
proofPoints: BSP will eventually regulate AI/GenAI use in banking
SAS responsible AI framework governs any model including LLMs
objections.whatNotToSay: Don't dismiss GenAI as a fad — it's real. Position SAS as the governance layer for responsible GenAI.

phContext.localPresence: Strong Philippines presence. AWS PH team, local partners, Philippines availability zone (planned). Many PH enterprises already running production workloads on AWS.
knownCustomers: Multiple PH banks on AWS infrastructure
Several PH enterprises using SageMaker for basic ML
phContext.localPricing: Consumption-based. Can be bundled into existing EDP. Appears affordable but ML workloads at scale (GPU instances, real-time endpoints) get expensive quickly.
regulatoryGaps: No native BSP model governance workflows
AWS Fraud Detector is basic — not enterprise fraud management
Model documentation doesn't meet BSP examination format
Single-cloud lock-in conflicts with some PH enterprise multi-cloud strategies

partnerWhen: Customer is AWS-standardized and SAS runs on EKS
AWS team is supportive and sees SAS as complementary
Need is for industry solutions (fraud, AML, risk) that AWS lacks
Budget is additive to AWS infrastructure spend
competeWhen: AWS positioning SageMaker as complete ML platform replacement
Budget is locked into AWS EDP with no room for additional vendors
Customer's primary need is basic ML (not regulated industry solutions)
AWS sales team actively blocking SAS engagement

proofPoints.title: SAS + AWS in banking
proofPoints.detail: Multiple global banks run SAS Viya on AWS for fraud and risk — leveraging AWS infrastructure with SAS governance and industry solutions.

proofPoints.title: AWS Marketplace partnership
proofPoints.detail: SAS available on AWS Marketplace demonstrates strategic partnership — not competition. Joint go-to-market in regulated industries.

proofPoints.title: Build vs. buy for fraud on AWS
proofPoints.detail: Building enterprise fraud management from SageMaker components: 12-18 months, $2-5M in engineering. SAS Fraud Management deploys in 8-12 weeks.
